filed.title: [REVIEW] Cross-Origin Policies: scored by presence only — `unsafe-none` / `cross-origin` get full credit
filed.finding: checkCrossOriginPolicies in src/rules.ts:145-164 scores COOP/COEP/CORP by presence only, awarding full 5/5 'good' status to the most permissive opt-out values (COOP: unsafe-none, COEP: unsafe-none, CORP: cross-origin) with no findings or recommendations.

filed.title: [REVIEW] Referrer-Policy: `no-referrer-when-downgrade` classified as "strong" — awards full score for the historical browser default that leaks full URLs cross-origin
filed.finding: checkReferrerPolicy in src/rules.ts:113 includes 'no-referrer-when-downgrade' in its strongValues list, awarding 10/10 and status 'good' to a policy that sends the full URL including path and query string to every cross-origin HTTPS server — the value Chrome replaced as its default in 2020 specifically because it was too leaky.

runner_ups.finding: CSP wildcard regex /(?:default-src|script-src)\s+\*/i only matches when '*' is the first source token; 'default-src 'self' *' is not flagged.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score; subsumed by broader CSP-evaluator-style follow-up to #5

runner_ups.finding: checkCSP does not recognize Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only header; report-only deployments are treated as if no CSP exists.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score; needs design discussion on whether report-only should count for points

runner_ups.finding: HSTS check awards full credit (preload bonus) when 'preload' directive is present even if max-age < 63072000 (2 years), which is the minimum the actual hstspreload.org submission requires.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score; smaller real-world consequence (sites are not auto-added to the preload list by the header alone)

runner_ups.finding: fetchHeaders uses method: 'HEAD' which causes false-missing readings on sites that set security headers conditionally on GET (e.g., HTML-only middleware).
checkCSP does not flag missing base-uri directive, leaving <base> injection silently bypassing script-src 'self'.
checkPermissionsPolicy always returns a non-empty recommendations array even when status is 'good' (score 10) — every correctly configured site sees 'Fix: Set Permissions-Policy to...' in CLI output.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score than primary finding (7.25); low security severity; DX bug only

runner_ups.finding: CLI getVersion() in src/cli.ts uses require('../package.json') inside an ESM-type package — on Node 18/20, require is undefined in ESM context, so --version always prints 0.0.0 via the silent catch fallback.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score; no security impact; DX/correctness bug

clean_areas: package.json exports/main/types/bin fields are correctly configured
CI workflows (ci.yml, publish.yml, auto-tag.yml) are sound
X-Frame-Options check (rules.ts:73-91) — accepts DENY/SAMEORIGIN and CSP frame-ancestors fallback as expected
fetch.ts AbortController timeout implementation (added in PRs #9/#10) is correctly structured
analyze() / analyzeHeaders() API surface and TypeScript types are consistent
